tasks.title: Establish the clean lofi application shell
tasks.objective: Remove the legacy production-MVP runtime and leave a runnable lofi-only Node application shell for incremental implementation.
authority: Delete auth, credits, payments, dashboards, social promotion, uploads, old persistence/domain/services, legacy tests, and production-MVP migrations/runbooks.
Retain scripts, agent workflow, lofi planning artifacts, and a branded runnable shell.
Do not implement cohort domain, persistence, forms, interest, or email behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Only lofi shell modules remain under src.
Legacy product routes return 404.
Home, styles, and health routes work.
Environment and Render placeholders use lofi-specific names.
Old migrations, production runbooks, assets, and tests are removed.
npm run check passes.
verification: Run npm run check
Inspect repository tree for legacy runtime artifacts
Smoke test home and health routes
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the clean shell is runnable and ready for L001.
tasks.evidence: 2026-06-18: legacy runtime removed; lofi home/health/style shell and focused tests added; npm run check passed.
tasks.notes: Git history preserves all removed production-MVP code and documentation.

tasks.title: Define the lofi domain and validation policy
tasks.objective: Replace production-MVP domain assumptions with the anonymous lofi cohort, lifecycle, email, scheduling, quorum, and public-link rules.
authority: Do not introduce credits, maximum participants, images, social status, or authenticated link-authorization fields into the lofi domain.
Keep categories, audience, skill level, recurrence, duration, meeting count, and approved meeting hosts where reusable.
Do not change persistence, routes, or UI in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Cohorts require a normalized private creator email.
Quorum accepts integers from 1 through 15 with no participant maximum.
Expiry defaults to seven days and the first meeting must be later than expiry.
Meeting links accept only HTTPS links on the existing approved host allowlist.
Lifecycle helpers define active/expired state and hide a successful link after the final meeting ends.
Focused tests cover valid and rejected boundary cases.
verification: Run node --test tests/domain-validation.test.mjs
Run npm run lint
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the lofi domain contract is tested without integrating persistence or HTTP routes.

tasks.title: Add isolated lofi persistence and Supabase migration
tasks.objective: Persist anonymous lofi cohorts, interests, notification outcomes, and rate-limit data through reusable repositories and a migration that cannot mix with production-MVP tables.
authority: Create new table names prefixed cohort15_lofi_.
Do not alter, drop, read, or write existing cohort15 production-MVP tables.
Enforce unique normalized interest email per cohort at repository and database levels.
Keep service-role credentials server-only.
acceptance_criteria: A clean store persists cohorts and interests without users, credits, purchases, images, or social posts.
Database uniqueness prevents duplicate interest by cohort and normalized email.
Creator and participant emails remain non-public repository fields.
Notification delivery outcomes can be recorded without blocking submissions.
The new migration is isolated from the existing production migration.
verification: Run focused persistence and Supabase adapter tests
Inspect migration for cohort15_lofi_ table isolation
Run npm run lint
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when isolated data access is tested; do not build forms or deploy a live database.
tasks.notes: Uses a new Supabase project in production.

tasks.title: Build anonymous cohort creation
tasks.objective: Allow any visitor to create a cohort with the retained form fields, mandatory private creator email, no auth, and lightweight abuse controls.
authority: Do not introduce auth, credits, image upload, or social outbox behavior into creation.
Accept creator email privately and normalize it.
Limit accepted cohort creations to five per IP per rolling hour.
Redirect successful creation to the cohort detail page.
Do not implement interest submission or email delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Creation works without a session.
The form keeps current content/schedule fields except image and maximum participants and adds creator email.
Friendly email-use and approved-link safety notes are visible.
The browser timezone is captured automatically and submitted meeting time is converted correctly.
Honeypot submissions and requests over the creation limit are rejected.
A successful request redirects to its public detail page without displaying creator email.
verification: Run focused create service and route tests
Run npm run lint
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when anonymous creation and redirect work locally; leave notifications to L006.
tasks.notes: Five accepted creations per IP per hour.

tasks.title: Build the landing page, listing, and lifecycle views
tasks.objective: Turn the current early-interest design into the lofi landing page with the full public listing, active-first sorting, filters, local meeting times, and safe link visibility.
authority: Reuse the supplied page's visual language and Google Analytics ID G-LF22TLDSBV.
Show all listings by default with active listings before expired listings.
Support All, Active, and Expired filters.
Never render email addresses.
Do not implement form mutations in this task.
acceptance_criteria: The home page explains the product and exposes Create a cohort and browsing actions.
The full cohort listing appears on the landing page.
All/Active/Expired filters work and default All sorts active first.
Exact interest count and quorum progress are public.
Meeting times are labeled and rendered in each visitor's local timezone.
Meeting links obey quorum and final-meeting visibility rules.
Expired listings remain visible.
verification: Run focused event browsing and UI tests
Verify rendered HTML contains the Google Analytics measurement ID
Run npm run lint
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when read-only public pages match the lofi contract.
tasks.notes: The separate /cohorts route may remain as a redirect or compatible view.

tasks.title: Build anonymous interest and quorum unlock
tasks.objective: Collect email-only interest without auth, prevent duplicates and creator self-interest, and unlock the public meeting link at quorum.
authority: Normalize email before comparison and persistence.
Reject creator email and duplicate cohort/email pairs.
Limit accepted interest submissions to ten per IP per rolling hour.
Close interest submission once quorum is met.
Do not send emails in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Open below-quorum cohorts show one email field and one interest button.
Friendly email-use consent text and a honeypot are present.
Creator and duplicate email submissions do not increment counts.
Quorum is based only on accepted interests.
Reaching quorum makes the approved meeting link public immediately.
The interest form is absent after quorum or expiry.
No email or IP address appears in public HTML or logs.
verification: Run focused show-interest and route tests
Run a create-to-quorum integration test
Run npm run lint
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the anonymous quorum flow works without notification delivery.
tasks.notes: Ten accepted interests per IP per hour.

tasks.title: Add Resend confirmation and quorum notifications
tasks.objective: Send creator and participant confirmations plus quorum-met notifications through a small Resend adapter.
authority: Use updates@cohort15.com as sender and cohort15dotcom@gmail.com as reply-to.
Do not send expiry emails or marketing broadcasts.
Do not expose the API key or recipient list in logs.
A provider failure must not roll back an accepted cohort or interest submission.
acceptance_criteria: Creators receive a creation confirmation.
Participants receive an interest confirmation.
At quorum, the creator and all accepted participants receive a quorum notification with public cohort/meeting details.
Repeated requests do not duplicate quorum notifications.
Delivery failures are recorded safely and submissions remain accepted.
Tests use a fake adapter and make no live email calls.
verification: Run focused email adapter and notification tests
Run create/interest integration tests with a fake email provider
Run npm run lint
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when transactional notification behavior is tested; do not configure the live Resend dashboard.
tasks.notes: Live domain and key setup is L009.

tasks.title: Isolate lofi production configuration and deployment
tasks.objective: Make the lofi runtime start with only its own Render, Supabase, Resend, analytics, and app URL settings and no production-MVP service dependencies.
authority: Name the Render service cohort15-lofi-mvp and target this branch until merge/deployment policy changes.
Require lofi-specific Supabase URL/service key and Resend key only in production.
Do not add production requirements for Supabase Auth, sessions, admin, Stripe, social publishing, or uploads.
Do not create provider resources or change DNS.
acceptance_criteria: Production startup cannot silently use local/demo persistence.
Production config has no auth, Stripe, credit, social, or upload requirement.
The service binds to Render's host/port and exposes a health endpoint.
Configuration documentation clearly forbids credential reuse with the production MVP.
Render and env examples are lofi-specific.
verification: Run focused runtime configuration tests
Run npm run check
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when local code/config is deployment-ready; provider dashboards remain a human task.
tasks.notes: External setup is documented in docs/human-tasks/lofi-mvp-launch.md.

tasks.title: Complete lofi privacy, abuse, and end-to-end verification
tasks.objective: Verify the complete lofi behavior and remove inactive production-MVP runtime surfaces that could expose stale flows.
authority: Remove obsolete auth, dashboard, credit, payment, image, and social routes from the lofi runtime.
Delete source files only after proving they are unused by the lofi app.
Do not broaden product scope or add an admin UI.
acceptance_criteria: Automated tests cover create, browse, filter, interest, duplicate rejection, creator rejection, quorum unlock, seven-day expiry, final-meeting link hiding, and local-time labels.
Tests cover both IP limits and honeypots.
Rendered pages and logs do not expose emails, IPs, secrets, or pre-quorum links.
Auth, credit, payment, social, image, dashboard, and admin routes remain absent from the lofi runtime.
npm run check passes.
verification: Run npm run check
Run a local browser smoke test for primary mobile and desktop flows
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when local launch verification passes and only external setup remains.
tasks.notes: This is the local launch gate.

tasks.title: Create isolated provider resources and deploy
tasks.objective: Complete the documented human setup for a new Supabase project, Render service, Resend sender domain/key, and cohort15.com cutover.
write_scope: provider dashboards and DNS only; repository changes only for discovered configuration corrections
authority: Follow the human setup checklist and never paste credentials into chat or commit them.
Do not select or modify the existing production-MVP Supabase project or Render service.
Keep the Netlify site available until the Render generated URL passes smoke verification.
acceptance_criteria: A new Supabase project contains only the lofi schema.
A new Render service deploys the lofi branch with lofi-only environment variables.
Resend verifies cohort15.com and sends from updates@cohort15.com.
The generated Render URL passes health and primary-flow checks.
cohort15.com resolves to the new Render service over HTTPS.
verification: Perform the non-secret checks in docs/human-tasks/lofi-mvp-launch.md
Record only provider resource names, URLs, and pass/fail status
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the domain cutover is verified or report the exact human/provider blocker.
tasks.notes: This task necessarily requires the user to operate provider dashboards.

tasks.title: Run production lofi smoke test
tasks.objective: Verify the deployed cohort15.com experience and database/email side effects without touching production-MVP resources.
authority: Use test email addresses controlled by the operator.
Do not expose test emails in committed evidence.
Do not mark complete if data appears in a production-MVP database.
acceptance_criteria: Landing, creation, detail redirect, interest, quorum unlock, filters, expiry rendering, and meeting-link timing pass in production.
Creator, participant, and quorum emails arrive with correct sender/reply-to.
Supabase rows appear only in the isolated lofi project.
Analytics loads and HTTPS/domain routing are correct.
No launch-blocking privacy or abuse failure remains.
verification: Run the production checklist in docs/human-tasks/lofi-mvp-launch.md
Run npm run check after any repository fix
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the production smoke passes or launch blockers are documented precisely.
